final_answer.answer: All HTTP calls flow through apiClient (src/lib/apiClient.ts) which wraps fetch() with a configurable BASE_URL. The endpoints are: POST /auth/login (loginUser, triggered by LoginForm), GET /discussions (fetchDiscussions, triggered by DiscussionList mount), POST /discussions/:id/comments (addComment, triggered by CommentForm submit), GET /teams (fetchTeams, triggered by TeamList mount), GET /users/:id (fetchUser, triggered by UserProfile mount). Additional unused endpoints: POST /auth/logout, GET /auth/me, POST /discussions, GET /discussions/:id/comments, POST /teams, PUT /users/:id.

ranked_files.reason: Central transport layer — all HTTP calls go through this

final_answer.answer: The data flow for the Discussions feature is: 1) The Discussion type (src/types/discussion.ts) defines the shape: id, title, content, authorId, createdAt. 2) DiscussionService.fetchDiscussions() calls apiClient.get('/discussions') which returns Discussion[]. 3) DiscussionList mounts, useEffect fires, calls fetchDiscussions().then(setDiscussions) to store the array in component state. 4) DiscussionList renders a <ul> with <li> per discussion showing d.title and d.content. The entire chain: type definition → service function → HTTP transport → component state → JSX render.

final_answer.answer: Five components have unhandled async operations. LoginForm.handleSubmit calls await loginUser() without try/catch — if the API rejects, the error is unhandled. CommentForm.handleSubmit calls await addComment() without try/catch. DiscussionList, TeamList, and UserProfile all use useEffect with .then() but no .catch() — if the fetch fails, the promise rejection is unhandled. Only authService.getCurrentUser has a try/catch (catches and returns null), but it is unused by any component.

ranked_files.reason: handleSubmit has await loginUser() without try/catch — user sees no error on login failure
handleSubmit has await addComment() without try/catch — user sees no error on comment failure
useEffect fetchDiscussions().then() has no .catch() — silent failure on load
useEffect fetchTeams().then() has no .catch() — silent failure on load
useEffect fetchUser().then() has no .catch() — silent failure on load

final_answer.answer: Every feature service directly imports apiClient. If its method signatures change, all 5 service files break: authService (loginUser, logoutUser, getCurrentUser), discussionService (fetchDiscussions, createDiscussion), commentService (fetchComments, addComment), teamService (fetchTeams, createTeam), userService (fetchUser, updateUser). The UI components are indirectly affected because they call these service functions. The most critical path is authService.loginUser → LoginForm.handleSubmit since it is the only write-path currently wired into the UI alongside commentService.addComment → CommentForm.handleSubmit.
